summary_one_line: Input must contain a PNG chunk whose 4-byte chunk-name FOURCC equals 'sPLT' so chunk_name == png_sPLT is true.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A chunk header whose 4-byte chunk-name field is the literal FOURCC 'sPLT' (0x73 0x50 0x4C 0x54), so the 32-bit equality test chunk_name == png_sPLT resolves true at the else-if chain at pngread.c:902.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: cmplog intercepts the chunk_name == png_sPLT integer comparison, logs the constant png_sPLT operand into the CmpLogObserver buffer, and I2SRandReplace splices that 4-byte constant into a chunk-name offset of the input; the cmplog winner resolves the branch 9/10 with hitcount 21 vs the naive loser's 0, and its ancestor chain contains I2S-floor dash rows.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive has only havoc/token mutation and must blindly produce the exact 4-byte 'sPLT' magic by chance; it never resolves the branch (resolved=0/10, blocked=10/10) and its branch hitcount stays 0.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver entry for the chunk_name comparison and substitutes the logged png_sPLT constant directly into the input chunk-name bytes, which naive (havoc/token only, no CMP introspection) cannot do.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A chunk header whose 4-byte chunk-name field is the literal FOURCC 'sPLT' (0x73 0x50 0x4C 0x54), reached by incrementally matching more bytes of the png_sPLT constant in the chunk_name == png_sPLT comparison.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: value_profile records the chunk_name vs png_sPLT comparison into CMP_MAP keyed by operand-distance bucket; each input that matches more prefix bytes of png_sPLT adds a new bucket and is retained as corpus, giving a gradient that climbs to the full 4-byte match; the value_profile winner resolves 10/10 with branch hitcount 3 vs the naive loser's 0.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive's edge-only feedback gives no signal for partial chunk-name matches, so it has no gradient toward the 'sPLT' FOURCC and never resolves the branch (resolved=0/10, blocked=10/10).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient feedback rewards inputs whose chunk_name is closer (more matching bytes) to the png_sPLT constant, preserving partial-match seeds as stepping stones; naive lacks this distance feedback.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked branch is the equality test chunk_name == png_sPLT at pngread.c:902, true side globally blocked.
Globally blocked side is the true branch.
Taking the true side dispatches to png_handle_sPLT, the winner-only resolved line.
cmplog wins over naive on this branch with the I2S technique delta.
The cmplog winner resolves 9/10 with branch hitcount 21 while the naive loser stays at 0.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=21  loser=0

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog adds I2SRandReplace, which scans the input for an operand match and substitutes the other operand.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: naive has no I2S mutator and relies on havoc/token only.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile wins over naive on this branch with the value_profile technique delta.
The value_profile winner resolves 10/10 with branch hitcount 3 while the naive loser stays at 0.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=3  loser=0

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile retains inputs whose CMP operands are closer to matching as new CMP_MAP buckets, giving a distance gradient.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
An input that produces a
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile has no I2SRandReplace, so its advantage comes from CMP_MAP feedback not substitution.
naive never resolves the branch across all 10 trials in both pairs.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: naive                            0       10          0  loser (value_profile vs value_profile); loser (I2S vs cmplog)

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 3901 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed 47d7e6a67984ab85` and observed I2S-floor dash rows (mutation_op = -) at depths 6 and 7 of the ancestor chain; the tool reports '2 ancestor dash row(s) at depth 6, 7', direct lower-bound evidence of an I2SRandReplace find in this cmplog winner's lineage.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating on chunk_name == png_sPLT in which cmplog shows no advantage over naive (equal resolve rate) would refute the I2S hypothesis; if value_profile also fails to resolve when only partial-prefix CMP_MAP gradient is available, the gradient hypothesis is refuted.

weakest_evidence_point: The byte-diff section concentrates on IHDR-region offsets (0x13-0x2e) not the sPLT chunk-name bytes themselves, so the seeds shown do not directly display the 'sPLT' FOURCC offset, leaving the exact substitution site inferred from source rather than observed in the dump.
